note: Official expectation model only - live integration NOT executed and NO compatibility/certification is claimed.
authMethod: Sign-in returns a credentials token sent as the 'X-Tableau-Auth' header. Preferred: Personal Access Token (PAT) via POST /api/{version}/auth/signin with { credentials: { personalAccessTokenName, personalAccessTokenSecret, site: { contentUrl } } }.

expectedRequestShape.requirement: API version in the path must match a version the server supports; subsequent calls send X-Tableau-Auth.

expectedSuccessResponseShape.http: 200 OK; signin returns { credentials: { token, site: { id }, user: { id } } }

expectedFailureResponseShape.http: 401001 invalid credentials / expired token  OR  401002 unauthorized  OR  404 site/resource not found  OR  405 version not supported

commonIntegrationFailure: 401 when the auth token expired (tokens are short-lived) or the PAT was rotated; 405/404 when the API version in the URL path is newer than the server supports; wrong site contentUrl yields an empty/forbidden result set.
diagnosticCommand: GET /api/{version}/serverinfo to discover supported API/product versions; re-run /auth/signin to refresh the token; confirm site contentUrl matches the target site.
preventionRule: Pin the API version to one the server reports via /serverinfo; refresh the X-Tableau-Auth token before its idle timeout; store PAT name+secret as secrets and rotate via config, never in code.
